Section Title: Welcome to Matthews Asia > Peeyush Mittal, CFA > Commentary > Outlook
Content:
While questions remain regarding the government’s commitment to infrastructure CapEx programs—historically one of the key drivers of the economy—we believe India has turned a corner.
The government and the central bank have made aggressive steps to encourage a recovery in consumer spending through rate cuts and tax reductions which may provide increasing tailwinds for the economy over time.
India’s economy is primarily domestically driven rather than characterized by thematic trends such as artificial intelligence (AI). As a result, challenges related to trade with the U.S. are expected to be less impactful relative to many peers.

results.title: Best Thematic Mutual Funds in India: Complete Guide for 2026
excerpts:
Section Title: Best Thematic Mutual Funds 2026: Complete List & Insights
Content:
Tanmay Srivastava
March 15, 2026 · 10,059 views
Investing in India can be both rewarding and challenging. While traditional mutual funds provide broad market exposure, thematic mutual funds, on the other hand, allow investors to focus on specific growth trends such as infrastructure, technology, renewable energy, or ESG-compliant companies.
Moreover, by investing in the best thematic mutual funds, you can align your portfolio with India’s evolving economic story and, at the same time, still enjoy diversification across sectors and companies.
This guide explains everything NRIs need to know about thematic funds, including how they work, their benefits, risks, and the step-by-step investment process.
 ... 
Section Title: ... > **Popular Types of Thematic Mutual Funds for NRIs**
Content:
| **Type of Fund** | **Focus Area / Example** |
| Dividend Yield Funds | Companies that pay consistent dividends (e.g., ITC, Coal India). |
| PSU Equity Funds | Government-owned enterprises benefiting from reforms and infrastructure push. |
| MNC Funds | Multinational companies with strong balance sheets and global operations (e.g., Nestlé, HUL). |
| Energy Funds | Renewable (solar, wind, green hydrogen) and conventional energy (oil & gas). |
| Consumption Funds | Consumer goods & services, tapping into India’s rising middle-class demand. |
| ESG Funds | Companies following Environmental, Social, and Governance principles. |
| Manufacturing Funds | Linked to infrastructure, metals, chemicals, construction, and engineering. |
 ... 
Section Title: ... > **How NRIs Can Invest in Thematic Funds in India**
Content:
Investing in Indian thematic funds as an NRI requires a few steps:
[**Open an NRE or NRO Account** :](https://sbnri.app.link/rpuTzlitzWb)
**NRE Accounts:** Funds from abroad, fully repatriable.
**NRO Accounts:** Income earned in India, partial repatriation.
**Complete KYC** : Submit the following:
Passport with photo and address
PAN card
Recent photograph
Cancelled cheque or Indian bank statement
FATCA declaration
**Choose Investment Mode** :
Lump-sum investment or Systematic Investment Plan (SIP)
**Check Country-Specific Restrictions** : Some AMCs do not accept US or Canadian NRIs due to FATCA, but offline options may be available.
Section Title: Best Thematic Mutual Funds 2026: Complete List & Insights > **Risks to Consider**
Content:
**Concentration Risk:** Focused exposure means performance heavily depends on the chosen theme.
**Market Volatility:** Themes may underperform during certain market cycles.
**Regulatory Risks:** Government policy changes can impact sectors like energy or defense.
**Long-Term Horizon Required:** Some themes may take years to deliver expected returns.
**For more insights, check out our guide on [Best Indian Mutual Funds to Invest in 2025.](https://sbnri.com/blog/nri-mutual-fund/best-indian-mutual-funds-to-invest-in-2025-for-nris)**
 ... 
Section Title: Best Thematic Mutual Funds 2026: Complete List & Insights > **FAQs**
Content:
**Q5. Is thematic investing good?
** Yes, if done strategically. Allocating 5–10% of your portfolio to the best thematic mutual funds can enhance diversification and growth potential while aligning with India’s growth story.
**Q6. Which type of mutual fund is a thematic fund?
** Thematic funds are equity-oriented mutual funds designed around a specific investment theme such as technology, infrastructure, or ESG.
**Q7. What are the 4 types of funds?** **
** The four common mutual fund types are:
Equity Funds
Debt Funds
Hybrid Funds
Thematic / Sector Funds
**Q8. Top 5 thematic mutual funds
** As of 2026, some of the top performing thematic mutual funds include:
ICICI Prudential Manufacturing Fund
Franklin India Opportunities Fund
Canara Robeco Consumer Trends Fund
ICICI Prudential Commodities Fund
Tata Business Cycle Fund

Section Title: Best International Mutual Funds in India 2026 > ... > **Key Selection Criteria in Points:**
Content:
**Fund Mandate and Geographical Focus:** Assess the fund's mandate, like does whether it targets a single country (e.g., US equity), a broad region (e.g., Europe), or offers global diversification. For core international exposure, stick to broad-based funds and avoid niche or thematic options. **Expense Ratio:** International funds typically charge higher Expense Ratios due to complex operations. Look for index or passive FoFs tracking established benchmarks (like the Nasdaq 100 or S&P 500), as they often have the lowest running costs. **Risk Appetite:** You must honestly assess your tolerance for market fluctuations and currency risk before investing globally. Choose funds aligned with your comfort level, favouring lower risk over high-volatility emerging or thematic funds.

These funds focus on specific themes such as technology, healthcare, sustainability, infrastructure, or digital transformation, rather than broad market sectors.
By aligning investments with powerful structural changes, thematic mutual funds aim to generate growth over time while allowing investors to express conviction in particular economic or social developments. results.title: Best International Mutual Funds in India for April 2026 by 3‑Year CAGR: Nippon India US Equity Opp Fund and More
excerpts:
Section Title: Best International Mutual Funds in India for April 2026 by 3‑Year CAGR: Nippon India US Equity Op...
Content:
Mutual fund returns tell 2 very different stories over the past year. While domestic equity funds struggled to move beyond the ~10–30% range, international mutual funds delivered substantial gains, with some even crossing the 100% mark.
Data from Value Research shows that global funds such as Nippon India Taiwan Equity and DSP World Gold Mining FoF led the charts. In contrast, the best-performing domestic options, largely PSU bank ETFs, delivered returns of around 29%, while broader categories like mid-cap and flexi-cap funds remained in low double digits.
Here are some options for you to consider while you plan on investing in international mutual funds.
 ... 
Section Title: ... > **Conclusion**
Content:
International mutual funds have clearly outpaced domestic peers in the past year, driven by strong global sectoral rallies and exposure to high-growth markets like the US and Taiwan. However, the return gap also highlights an uncomfortable truth: higher returns often come with higher concentration risk and volatility, as seen in funds with elevated tracking errors.
For investors, the takeaway is less about chasing triple-digit returns and more about building a balanced portfolio. Low-cost index funds tracking global benchmarks like the NASDAQ 100 and S&P 500 offer cost efficiency and diversification, while selective active funds can provide exposure to niche themes such as mining, energy, and healthcare.
